title: Marbella No Speedboat Hire Licence Costs and Tips 2026
html:
In Marbella No, renting a speedboat without a licence is straightforward when you book through a marketplace like BoatHire24, where prices for a half-day start at around 300 EUR. Depart from Puerto Banús or the Real Club de Marbella to explore the Costa del Sol. This article covers licence rules, costs, and the best boats for a day on the Mediterranean.

Do you need a licence to hire a speedboat in Marbella No?

In most cases, no licence is needed if the boat is under 15 horsepower and limited to a certain distance from shore. Spanish regulations allow unlicensed operation of small motorboats with engine power below 15 hp (11.2 kW), which includes many small speedboats and tenders. However, laws can vary slightly between autonomous regions, so the operator confirms exact requirements at the time of booking. Always check the listing details on the platform to ensure the boat meets your licence status.

Where to set off in Marbella No

The coastline around Marbella No offers several excellent launching points. Puerto Banús is the most famous, a marina lined with luxury yachts and boutiques. Real Club de Marbella is another well-equipped harbour with direct access to the sea. For a quieter start, consider Cabopino Marina in the east or the small port at San Pedro Alcántara. To the west, Estepona Marina provides a charming base. Nearby, the island of Isla de las Palomas near Cabopino is a popular stop, about 5 nautical miles from Marbella No. You can compare verified boat listings in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent

BoatHire24 lists a range of vessels suitable for unlicensed hire. Below are four popular choices. One option worth a look is the Olympic 490 for hire.

  1. Small speedboat (up to 15 hp) Ideal for coastal cruising; fits 4 passengers; from 300 EUR half-day.
  2. Bowrider (e.g., Olympic 490) Great for families; accommodates 5 people; from 350 EUR half-day.
  3. RIB (rigid inflatable boat) Versatile for watersports; takes 6 guests; from 400 EUR half-day.
  4. Small motor yacht (e.g., Azimut 32) Comfortable for longer trips; sleeps 4; from 800 EUR full day.
  5. Catamaran (e.g., Lagoon 380) Stable and spacious; up to 10 guests; from 900 EUR full day (requires licence for some models).

For a licenced or higher-powered boat, you can always compare verified boat listings on the platform to find the perfect vessel. If you own a boat, you can rent out your boat and earn from spare days.

How much does it cost to hire a boat in Marbella No?

Costs vary by boat type and duration, but a half-day unlicensed speedboat typically ranges from 300 to 500 EUR. Below is a general price band breakdown in EUR.

  • Shared tour: usually 50, 80 EUR per person for a 2-hour trip
  • Small motorboat half-day: 300, 500 EUR without licence
  • Catamaran full-day: 900, 1,200 EUR (often licensed skipper included)
  • Crewed yacht full-day: 1,500, 3,000 EUR with skipper and crew

For specific models like the Olympic 490, you can check rates on the Olympic 490 for hire page. To see how booking works, visit the boat search.

Season and local tips

High season runs from June to September, when the Levante wind can pick up in the afternoon. This easterly wind increases sea state, so mornings are calmer. Use the boat search to find available dates and compare options.

Hire a boat early in the morning to avoid the Levante wind; the sea is usually flat until midday, giving you the best conditions for exploring coves near Cabopino.

In 2026, booking in advance is wise for peak weeks in July and August.

At a glance

  • Price band: 300, 1,200 EUR half-day / full-day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 2, 8 hours
  • Guest capacity: 4, 10 (varies by boat)
  • Currency: EUR (Euro)

Questions about boat rental in Marbella No

What licence do I need for a speedboat in Marbella No?

For boats under 15 hp, no licence is required, but the operator confirms the exact requirements for each boat.

Can I rent a boat in Marbella No without a licence?

Yes, many small speedboats and RIBs are available for unlicensed hire, provided you follow local distance limits.

How much is fuel for a boat in Marbella No?

Fuel costs around 20, 40 EUR per hour for a small speedboat, often charged after the trip based on usage.

What is the best time of day to rent a boat in Marbella No?

Early morning is best for calm seas; afternoons can be windy due to the Levante wind.

After reading this guide, you can use the boat search on BoatHire24 to find available options and book easily. If you own a boat in Marbella No, consider listing it to reach thousands of travellers; rent out your boat through the platform.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella No and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
